findings.description: Two open PROPs (PROP-003, PROP-004) were filed AFTER their target bugs were already fixed (by 30 and 21 minutes respectively). PROP-003 was then re-escalated in 9 consecutive reports because the escalation loop used md5-of-workspace-vs-main as its verification — which matches whenever both are fixed. This is the exact cautionary tale captured in tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md lines 100. The second PROP to hit this pattern (PROP-004) had the same root cause: no verification_pattern field.
findings.evidence: PROP-003 created 2026-04-08T20:30; fix commit f094909 landed 2026-04-08T19:59:24Z (30 min earlier). PROP-004 created 2026-04-09T10:45; fix commit 0734e78 landed 2026-04-09T10:24:02Z (21 min earlier). Both PROPs lacked verification_pattern; md5 matched through every escalation.

suggested_fix.replace: Make verification_pattern a HARD REQUIREMENT in the PROP template, and have Step 2b refuse to write a PROP without one — error out with a self finding. Current wording ('Always include') is advisory; the last two PROPs both shipped without it.
suggested_fix.rationale: The cautionary-tale paragraph captures the lesson but doesn't block the behavior. Enforce at write-time, not review-time.

findings.description: Decider no-op rate ~40% over the last 10 runs (Opus, 4h cadence). In a quiet-dome regime (3 consecutive quiet polls, dome site in maintenance since V51.1 Australia Validation push) the decider frequently wakes to find: 0 new curmudgeon reviews in digest, 0 new expansions, 0 external reports, 0 poll changes. Substantive work always exists when one of those triggers fires, but the pre-roll setup (clone, pull, read prompt, read digest, read trackers) is paid every run regardless.
findings.evidence: 10 most recent daily-report-*.json scanned by counting curmudgeon_reviews_processed + expansions_processed + internal_issues.new_issues_created + code_analysis_updates. SUBST runs: 6 (19:07, 15:09, 04:18, 18:32, 11:08, 10:30). NOOP runs: 4 (23:06, 17:27, 07:10, 19:40 prev day). 40% no-op on Opus at 4h cadence = ~4 Opus invocations/day doing pure discovery → nothing.

suggested_fix.replace: Haiku pre-flight gate that runs before each decider invocation: reads digest-reviews.js output + expansion-tracker.json + latest-poll-summary.txt + external-reports count, returns a 1-bit 'work_available' signal. Scheduled task reads the signal and skips Opus decider when false. Savings: ~4 Opus invocations/day × ~60% of cycles the dome is quiet ≈ 2-3 Opus runs/day saved at the cost of 6 Haiku runs/day.
suggested_fix.rationale: Quiet-dome is the current steady state and likely to persist until the author pushes V52. The decider's prompt is self-aware enough that it already reports 'no new digest entries' runs as its first Step. The signal to skip is mechanical.

findings.prop_candidate: PROP-005 (deferred to next Mode 3 run — this run's budget went to PROP verification and self-finding).

findings.description: Counter-argument to the decider Haiku gate proposal (required by CLAUDE evenhandedness): the decider's no-op runs are not pure waste. (a) Step 1i poll-summary triage still cross-checks against open-issues.json even when no new findings exist, catching drift from earlier runs. (b) The decider's processed-reviews ledger maintenance is cheap but needs to happen even on quiet days. (c) Gating Opus on Haiku output introduces a new failure mode: Haiku mis-reads the digest and the decider never runs. These are all real but secondary to the straight cost math — still worth including in any PROP-005 tradeoff section.
findings.evidence: Decider prompt Step 1i, Step 2c ledger update. Decider already writes a 'pipeline_status' block even when no substantive work is done, which is evidence that quiet-run bookkeeping has some value.

previous_followup.finding: PROP-003: digest-reviews.js processedWinIds legacy fallback (9th consecutive escalation as of last run)
previous_followup.evidence: Manual verification: `grep -q 'processedWinIds' build-scripts/digest-reviews.js` returns no matches. Line 143 reads `const isProcessed = processedSet.has(file);` — exactly the replacement PROP-003 proposed. GitHub API confirms fix commit f094909 on 2026-04-08T19:59:24Z ('Remove dead processedWinIds legacy fallback from digest-reviews.js'). PROP-003 was created 30 minutes AFTER this commit — it was born already-fixed. Escalation history was an artifact of md5-based verification against main (which matched because both were fixed). PROP-003 file updated in place this run: status=implemented, implemented_at=2026-04-08T19:59:24Z, verification_pattern added.

build.js lines 217-238 contain dynamic session detection: `const sessionMatch = (process.cwd().match(/\/sessions\/([^/]+)/) || [])[1]` + /sessions scan fallback + visible `⚠️  Workspace sync skipped` warning at line 329. The hardcoded busy-cool-mayer path is gone. Fix commit: 0734e78 ('Close PROP-004: dynamic session detection in build.js workspace sync') on 2026-04-09T10:24:02Z — 21 minutes BEFORE PROP-004 was filed (10:45). This run's FUSE hash check is the first downstream verification: 5/5 files match main. PROP-004 file updated in place: status=implemented, verification_pattern added.

previous_followup.evidence: Social run 2 summary (11:09Z today) reads: 'Nothing has changed since run 1 earlier today'. Dome machine-readable layer byte-identical to run 1. No new TikTok/Twitter/Reddit activity. 'Actions taken this run: none (steady-state).' Social runs daily on Sonnet — the second daily run of a quiet day is a prime Haiku-gate candidate alongside the decider. Bundling into the same PROP-005 makes sense: one pre-flight gate script, two consumers.

agent_efficiency.note: This run produced: 2 PROP lifecycle closures (PROP-003, PROP-004), 1 self-finding (PROPs without verification_pattern), 1 new efficiency observation (decider + social Haiku gate). Previous 9 runs re-escalated a fixed bug due to bad verification — that is pure self-waste and argues for enforcing verification_pattern at PROP-write time.
agent_efficiency.self_waste_estimate: 9 of last 10 runs spent token budget on re-escalating an already-fixed PROP. That is ~30-50% of tinker's Mode 1/2 overhead on a dead finding. The self-finding in this report is a harder fix than the prompt edit — it requires write-time enforcement, not just better verification discipline.

proposals_queued.summary: Haiku pre-flight gate for decider (and optionally social) on quiet-dome days
proposals_queued.prerequisites: Need to measure 7-day decider no-op rate (not just last 10 runs) and social 14-day no-op rate to validate savings. Also need to design the 'work_available' signal schema so it survives the FUSE mount.

cost_engineering.cumulative_estimated_savings: PROP-003: prevented Cycle 3+ under-coverage (not a token saving — a correctness fix). PROP-004: eliminated silent workspace-sync skip across ephemeral sessions (not token — correctness + downstream staleness prevention). Both are quality fixes, not cost fixes. Tinker has not yet landed a pure cost PROP — PROP-005 would be the first.

recommendations.description: Write PROP-005: Haiku pre-flight gate for decider and social on quiet-dome days. Include counter-arguments (Step 1i triage, ledger maintenance, Haiku misread failure mode).

Update monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md Step 2a to make verification_pattern a hard requirement: refuse to write a PROP without one, emit a self finding instead. Prevents the PROP-003/004 pattern from recurring.
Next tinker run (likely Mode 3): audit open-issue health (13 open) — nothing looked urgent this run but no actual audit was performed. Check for aged-out / repeated-deferral patterns.

notes_to_future_tinker: If you see PROP-003 or PROP-004 flagged as STILL_BROKEN in a future report, read their actual_result field — they are closed and have explicit grep-based verification_patterns. Re-escalation means the pattern returned STILL_BROKEN, which would be a genuine regression in digest-reviews.js or build.js respectively. The md5-vs-main verification pattern from earlier runs is disallowed.
